page.content: The analysis of participant characteristics reveals critical insights into the demographic profile of the study population. Notably, the mean age of participants is 55.1 years, with a standard deviation of 10.7, indicating a diverse age range that encompasses both younger and older adults. This age distribution is significant as it allows for a more comprehensive understanding of the factors influencing the study's primary outcomes.

lines.text: The analysis of participant characteristics reveals critical insights into the demographic profile of the study population. Notably,
the mean age of participants is 55.1 years, with a standard deviation of 10.7, indicating a diverse age range that encompasses
both younger and older adults. This age distribution is significant as it allows for a more comprehensive understanding of the
factors influencing the study's primary outcomes.

cells.cell_text: Distance from lesion to the pleural surface, mm

page.content: In examining the characteristics of the participants, it is essential to consider how age may intersect with other variables, such as health status and socioeconomic factors, which are crucial in interpreting the data effectively. The mean (SD) metric provides a foundational framework for assessing the variability within the age group, highlighting the potential for differing experiences and outcomes based on age-related factors. Furthermore, understanding these characteristics is vital for tailoring interventions and recommendations that address the unique needs of this population. As the study progresses, continued analysis of these characteristics will enhance the robustness of the findings and facilitate a nuanced discussion around age-related implications in the context of the research objectives.
